top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 149, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Amstel Gold Race Toerversie's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
10,410ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
70ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Amstel Gold Race Toerversie's steep short climbs through rolling Limburg hills forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 149 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Amstel Gold Race Toerversie. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 10,410ft of climbing on steep short climbs through rolling Limburg hills does to your power file on race day.
At Amstel Gold Race Toerversie, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
149 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 149 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on steep short climbs through rolling Limburg hills. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 149 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Amstel Gold Race Toerversie’s 149 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 149 miles of steep short climbs through rolling limburg hills in Valkenburg, South Limburg, Netherlands.
